titleStmt.title: Letter from John Thompson to the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, 10 October 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Thompson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from John Thompson (1867-1936) to the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland (RCPI) requesting permission to use the college's hall for a series of lectures on the Roman Empire.
Aside from being secretary of the Classical Association Thompson was the headmaster if Dublin High School and one of the founding members of the Dublin French Society. He was also a member of the Royal Irish Academy.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
<head.The Classical Association of Ireland
President for 1915 & 1916 : Prof. J. I. BEARE M.A., F.T.C.D.
Hon. Treasurer : R. A. S. MACALISTER, Esq., M.A. Litt.D., F.S.A., Newlands, Clonskeagh, Co. Dublin.
Hon. Secretary : J. THOMPSON, Esq., M.A., M.R.I.A., 40 Harcourt Street, Dublin. Oct 10 1916 To the Registrar
Royal College of Physicians, Dear Sir Your Council have in former years very kindly granted to
our Association the use of it's your hall for courses of
lectures on Classical subjects & as we are proposing to
hold a course this winter on The Roman Empire I have
been requested to ask whether your Council would
again do us the honour of allowing us the use of your
hall. We propose to have four lectures, two before &
two after Christmas & if your Council are willing, we
can arrange the dates later. I am yours faithfully
J. Thompson
Hon. Sec. Dates arranged
Wednesday 29. Nov., 13 Dec., 28 Feb. and 14 March.
